sections.html: This course uses a required textbook that aligns with CompTIA Security+ certification objectives. The hands-on labs bring the textbook concepts to life.

left.value: Principles of Computer Security: CompTIA Security+ and Beyond

sections.label: How to Access
items: UTSA Bookstore — Physical or digital; may be included in your course materials fee. Check with the bookstore.
McGraw-Hill Connect — Digital access with interactive features. Check Canvas for the course-specific access link.
Amazon / Other Retailers — Search by ISBN for used copies or alternative retailers. Always verify you’re getting the 6th edition.

sections.label: Chapter Coverage by Module
paragraphs:
ModuleTopicChapters
Module 1Threat Intelligence & ReconnaissanceCH 1, 2
Module 2Cryptography & AuthenticationCH 6, 11
Module 3Networks & DefenseCH 9, 13, 18
Module 4Threats & AttacksCH 15, 16
Module 5Infrastructure & Risk ManagementCH 10, 20

sections.label: How to Use the Textbook
items: Before each module: Read the assigned chapters to understand the concepts you’ll be practicing in the labs.
During labs: Reference specific sections when you need deeper explanation of a technique or tool.
For reports: Cite the textbook when explaining methodology or referencing security principles. It counts toward your 10-citation requirement.
For exams: Module exams draw from both textbook content and lab experience.
sections.tip: “If we don’t touch it in a lab, we don’t assign the reading.” Every assigned chapter directly supports the hands-on work.

sections.html: The textbook is your reference manual. The labs are where you learn.
